_meta.description: SageReasoning Compliance Pipeline Audit Log — append-only record of every pipeline run

runs.trigger: Data hygiene violation identified: founder practitioner profile in scope during Phase B infrastructure design session
runs.decision: Founder chose Option 1 (recuse from schema design visibility). RLS policies and vulnerability_flag schema to be designed from first principles with zero individual profiles visible. Founder reviews and approves finished SQL per CCP brief approval gate.
runs.rationale: Two risks identified: (1) design contamination — RLS policies shaped by one user's data encode implicit assumptions about that data shape, creating blind spots for other users; (2) independence principle violation (R20b) — founder simultaneously designing the system, being the sole user whose data it protects, and having his profile shape the design. Corrective process: first-principles design from role definitions and data sensitivity tiers, validated against multiple user archetypes using synthetic data only.
corrective_actions: Phase B CCP session proceeds with zero practitioner profiles loaded
Design inputs are abstract role definitions and data sensitivity tiers only
Validation uses nine user archetypes with synthetic data
First-principles design document produced for founder review before any SQL

runs.trigger: Phase B CCP session — vulnerability_flag table deployed and verified

design_decisions.4a: Database view for reviewer identity masking
design_decisions.4c: CASCADE delete for R17c genuine deletion (flagged for P3 legal review)

runs.data_hygiene: Enforced. Zero individual profiles loaded. First-principles design from role definitions and data sensitivity tiers. Document: /compliance/Phase-B-first-principles-design.md
runs.founder_approval: Item 1 (schema) and Item 2 (RLS policies) explicitly approved per CCP brief §(e)

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: EU AI Act — Art. 6 Classification Guidelines
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: EU AI Office published Art. 6 classification guidelines in February 2026, specifying practical implementation of high-risk classification rules with examples of high-risk and non-high-risk AI systems.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: EU AI Act — Digital Omnibus High-Risk Deadline Postponement
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: European Commission proposed via Digital Omnibus Act to postpone high-risk AI system obligations (originally Aug 2026) by up to 16 months. Backstop dates: Dec 2027 for Annex III, Aug 2028 for Annex I. Must be adopted before Aug 2, 2026 to take effect.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: EU AI Act — Art. 50 Code of Practice v2
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: Second Draft Code of Practice for marking and labelling AI-generated content published March 2026, establishing technical standards for watermarking and synthetic media detection.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: EU Digital Omnibus — GDPR Amendments for AI
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: Proposed GDPR amendments would allow legitimate interest basis for AI development/operation with enhanced safeguards, and new exemption for residual special-category data processing during AI training/testing.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: Australian Privacy Act — Automated Decision-Making Transparency
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024 (passed Dec 2024) introduces automated decision-making transparency obligations effective 10 December 2026. Businesses using AI to make decisions significantly affecting individuals must disclose in privacy policies.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: Australian Privacy Act — Children's Online Privacy Code
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: OAIC developing Children's Online Privacy Code under the 2024 amendments, to be registered by 10 December 2026. Sets obligations for digital services serving children under 18.
National AI Plan (Dec 2025) confirmed VAISS remains voluntary. No mandatory conversion. Australian AI Safety Institute (AISI) becoming operational early 2026.

AI-related digital market conduct included in broader enforcement priorities. No direct enforcement actions against AI evaluation services. Monitoring AI-enabled harms including ghost websites and false representations.

ASIC Moneysmart published consumer guidance on using AI for financial information (March 2026). Research shows 18% of Gen Z Australians using AI for financial guidance. ASIC emphasises existing AFSL obligations apply fully to AI-powered tools.

regulatory_changes_found.regulation: NIST AI RMF — Cyber AI Profile
regulatory_changes_found.change_summary: NIST developing Cyber AI Profile (initial public draft expected 2026). AI RMF 1.1 addenda and expanded profiles expected through 2026. Core RMF 1.0 unchanged.
New practitioner-oriented implementation guide published February 2026. No updates to the standard itself. Useful for gap analysis toward future certification.

impact_assessments.rationale: Must assess whether agent trust layer/evaluation constitutes high-risk under Annex III. Classification guidelines now available for review.
If adopted, extends compliance window by up to 16 months. Reduces urgency but does not remove obligation.

impact_assessments.change: EU Art. 50 Code of Practice v2
impact_assessments.rationale: R3 disclaimer already satisfies transparency. Confirm alignment when final Code published.
Legitimate interest basis for AI may simplify GDPR compliance. Proposal stage only.
Privacy policy must be updated before 10 Dec 2026 to disclose automated decision-making. Deadline is firm.
Age verification mechanisms may need strengthening. Code not yet finalised.
No direct enforcement actions against AI evaluation services. Continue monitoring.
Reinforces need for sage-invest lawyer review. No new regulatory requirements.
Voluntary framework, draft stage. No action required.

actions_for_founder: PRIORITY: Review EU Art. 6 classification guidelines (Feb 2026) to confirm agent trust layer is NOT classified high-risk under Annex III
PRIORITY: Update privacy policy (privacy/page.tsx) to fully comply with APP 1.7 automated decision-making transparency before 10 Dec 2026
Obtain lawyer review of privacy policy before Dec 2026 deadline
Monitor Children's Online Privacy Code draft from OAIC — assess age verification requirements
Monitor EU Digital Omnibus legislative progress for high-risk postponement and GDPR amendments
Continue sage-invest AFSL lawyer review before marketplace listing (reinforced by ASIC March 2026 guidance)
